ops.title: Plugin binary installers are brew-first — an installed brew binary wins even when it trails the plugin pin; canonical script lives in repo-bootstrap

ops.body:
For cc plugin binary provisioning, the user wants ONE canonical installer script whose home is cc-skills/plugins/repo-bootstrap (templates/plugin/install-binary.sh, the opt-in `plugin` extra; landed via cc-skills PR #2), consumed by plugin-template and the fleet via a tiered fetch (local checkout → marketplace clone → raw.githubusercontent pinned to CC_SKILLS_REF, never floating main) and rolled out with `render.sh --sync-scripts` (cc-interact PR #1). Resolution is 6 arms: exact-pin exit / dev-build leave-alone / PATH-probe (inode-safe `-ef` exclusion of plugin bin) / brew install / durable data-dir payload reuse / sha256-verified bare-asset download into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/bin`. Brew-first: a brew-installed binary wins outright even when stale vs the pin (best-effort `brew upgrade`, then symlink — deliberately diverging from cc-notes). Versions compare v-STRIPPED: goreleaser release binaries print `v0.5.0` while brew formula builds stamp their own ldflags and print `0.5.0`. `plugin/bin/<name>` is only ever a symlink (plugin dir is ephemeral; DATA dir is durable). Consumer repos need two release-surface prerequisites: bare `<name>_<os>_<arch>` goreleaser assets and a `--version` flag (slop-cop lacked both — its PR #1 adds them).

**Why:** the user explicitly locked brew-first ("keep the brew-first approach") and repo-bootstrap as the canonical home ("cc-interact/plugin-template should not be the canonical place… it should grab it from there") across two plan review rounds.

**How to apply:** never author per-repo installer variants; render from the repo-bootstrap template ([[cc-skills-plugin-source]]) and roll fixes out via `render.sh --sync-scripts`. Don't "fix" brew-lag by preferring the static download.

Rollout completed 2026-07-06: every consumer (cc-context, cc-review, cc-runtime, slop-cop, cc-skills plugins/slop-cop) is stamped `@ed090c97704f984ff2caed8f9f224816237fe468` (the cc-skills merge sha, full 40 chars); only the template itself stays `@pending`. Two operational gotchas: (1) `--sync-scripts` tier 1 stamps `rev-parse HEAD` of the live ~/Code/cc-skills checkout — a checkout parked on a PR branch stamps an unreachable sha (that's how the fleet briefly got `@7f14361`); for deterministic re-renders force tier 3 with `env -i HOME=<empty-dir> PATH=/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin sh render.sh --sync-scripts <plugin-dir>`. (2) cc-context's rendered copy carries a LOCAL migration block (pre-v0.5.0 shim sniff + payload sweep) that a sync would silently delete — for cc-context, verify canonical-portion parity on a temp copy, then hand-edit only the stamp line. Windows support was dropped fleet-wide (user: "get rid of all windows stuff") — no `.ps1` twins anywhere, slop-cop goreleaser is darwin/linux only since v0.1.32.
